codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all 8 expected behaviors. IDW heatmap overlay renders via HeatmapRenderer after 3+ valid points at 70% opacity with standard green→yellow→red gradient. Toolbar Picker for 5 visualization types triggers re-render via didSet. Missing data overlay with context-specific hints displays correctly. 14 well-structured tests cover threshold behavior, type switching, missing data, and display names. Two non-blocking issues: duplicated displayName logic across ViewModel and View, and a weak assertion in the overlay-update test.

issues.description: visualizationDisplayName computed property duplicates the exact same switch-case logic found in HeatmapSurveyView.displayName(for:) at line 329. This display name mapping should live as a computed property on HeatmapVisualization in NetMonitorCore to avoid duplication and ensure consistency across the macOS and future iOS targets.

displayName(for:) is a private function duplicating the same visualization-to-string mapping already in the ViewModel's visualizationDisplayName. One source of truth should exist — preferably on the HeatmapVisualization enum itself.

heatmapOverlayUpdatesWhenPointAdded test does not actually verify the overlay changed after adding a 4th point. It asserts overlayBefore != nil and vm.heatmapOverlayImage != nil, but never compares them. Since CGImage is a reference type, comparing with !== would at least confirm a new image was generated. The test proves the overlay is non-nil but not that it was re-rendered with different data.

renderHeatmapOverlay() runs CPU-intensive IDW interpolation synchronously on @MainActor. With the default 200x200 grid and <=50 points this is acceptable (~2M operations), but as point counts grow or if resolution increases, this will block the UI thread. Consider offloading to Task.detached in a future pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker duplicated the visualization display name mapping in both the ViewModel (visualizationDisplayName computed property) and the View (displayName(for:) function). AGENTS.md has no convention about where enum display names should live. For enums defined in NetMonitorCore that need human-readable names in UI, the convention should be: add a public computed property on the enum in NetMonitorCore so both macOS and iOS targets share one source of truth.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: HeatmapSurveyViewModel.swift:156-167 and HeatmapSurveyView.swift:325-334 contain identical switch statements mapping HeatmapVisualization cases to strings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used rsync to sync files to mac-mini instead of git push + git pull due to known git credential error (-25308). The macos-ui-worker skill's Step 6 only documents the SSH+xcodebuild test command and doesn't mention rsync as a fallback when mac-mini git pull fails. The handoff's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges confirms: 'Skill could mention rsync as fallback when mac-mini git pull fails'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Used rsync to sync files to mac-mini instead of git push + git pull'. SKILL.md Step 6 only shows ssh mac-mini + xcodebuild test command with no fallback for git sync failures.

summary: Feature passes review. All 8 expected behaviors are implemented and tested: 3-point overlay threshold (with removal below 3), 70% opacity via HeatmapRenderer, 5 visualization types in toolbar Picker with didSet re-rendering, missing data overlay with context-specific hints, and appropriate empty states. 14 new tests added with comprehensive coverage. Two non-blocking issues noted: duplicate displayName logic (ViewModel + View both have the same switch) and a weak test assertion that doesn't verify the overlay image actually changed on re-render. Synchronous main-thread rendering is acceptable at current scale but should be monitored.
